business.headline: Sensex ends 332 points lower, Nifty below 23,800; Eternal down 2%Sensex ends 332 points lower, Nifty below 23,800; Eternal down 2%

Your manager didn't back you this appraisal cycle? Survey shows 1 in 3 felt sameThe nationwide survey found that 22% of employees said they received no support from their manager during appraisals, while another 10% said they were mostly on their own. Together, that means 32% of employees navigated the appraisal process without meaningful backing from their reporting manager.
Why are HCLTech shares rising even as the stock market trades in the red today?At the time of writing, HCL Technologies shares were trading at Rs 1,268.60, up 1.92%.

Why is Russia importing petrol from India despite being an oil giant?Russia has started importing petrol by sea from India to tackle shortages caused by Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil refineries and energy facilities, mentioned a report.

8th Pay Commission to hold Delhi consultations on Aug 7 & 10. What's next?The 8th Central Pay Commission has scheduled fresh Delhi consultations on August 7 and 10 for eligible employee associations that have already submitted memorandums. The move shows the panel is still gathering stakeholder views before framing recommendations on pay, pensions and allowances.

Will the July 31 ITR filing deadline be extended? Rumours vs realityWith only a few days left to file income tax returns, speculation over a possible extension is once again gathering pace. But is there any truth to the rumours?

Flipkart to launch food delivery service soon, taking on Zomato and SwiggyFlipkart Group CEO Kalyan Krishnamurthy confirmed that the company will launch its food delivery service within the next few weeks.

Infosys, Tech Mahindra, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell in early trade as the Wall Street technology selloff spread to Dalal Street. Rising crude, higher US yields and geopolitical worries added to expectations of near-term volatility.

Sensex down over 500 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayBenchmark indices extended their losses in early trade, with the BSE Sensex plunging 902.48 points, or 1.18%, to 75,488.91, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 256.15 points, or 1.07%, to 23,613.45 around 10:20 am, as rising crude oil prices and weak global cues continued to weigh heavily on investor sentiment.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade lower as Brent crude tops $100The BSE Sensex fell 585.21 points, or 0.77%, to 75,806.18, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 173.65 points, or 0.73%, to 23,695.95 around 9:30 am.

AI bill catches up with Wall Street: Tesla crashes 14%, Alphabet falls 7%Tesla and Alphabet shares fell sharply after earnings highlighted the rising cost of AI investment. The reaction signalled that Wall Street is demanding clearer returns, cash flow and profit visibility.

Adani denies reports of airline plans, says not evaluating entry into aviationAdani Enterprises has denied reports that it is evaluating an entry into the airline business. The clarification follows speculation over rule changes and the group's wider aviation ambitions.

Why India's biggest airlines are worried about Adani's aviation ambitionsAdani's possible airline entry has prompted opposition from IndiGo and Air India. Both carriers say airport owners should remain neutral because they control vital infrastructure.

Will US scrap 10% tariff on India? Key deadline ends todayIf no new announcement is made, imports from India and several other US trading partners will revert to the normal Most Favoured Nation (MFN) tariff structure that was in place before April 2, 2025.
Crude oil crosses $100 for first time in two months as US-Iran conflict intensifiesAt the time of writing, Brent Crude, the global oil benchmark, was trading at $100.14 a barrel, up 6.45%.

Infosys to roll out salary hikes from October, hire 20,000 freshersThe IT major said the salary hike cycle will be implemented in three parts. While the company did not share details of the percentage increase, it confirmed that the rollout will begin in October and continue into January for different employee groups.

Infosys posts 12% jump in Q1 profit, trims upper end of FY27 growth outlookThe IT major posted a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,769 crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, compared with Rs 6,921 crore in the same period last year. Revenue from operations rose 14% to Rs 48,211 crore, up from Rs 42,279 crore a year ago.

Infosys appoints Ashiss Kumar Dash as CEO-designateAccording to the company, Dash will begin his role as CEO-designate with immediate effect. The Board has also approved its intention to appoint him as the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director from April 1, 2027.

Crude oil nears $100: Why rising price is bad news for IndiaAt the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $98.28 per barrel, up 4.48%, reflecting growing concerns over the geopolitical situation in the Middle East.

Retaining users is really hard: Nithin Kamath on broking business' biggest challengeKamath said one of the biggest hurdles for stockbrokers is user retention. While many people open trading accounts, not all continue investing or trading for years.

entertainment.headline: Karavali review: Prajwal-Raj B Shetty's film offers thrills but feels incompleteRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 Karavali movie review: With beautiful visuals and intriguing ideas, Gurudatta Ganiga's coastal drama, starring Prajwal Devaraj with Raj B Shetty in an extended cameo, offers moments of genuine thrill but ultimately feels incomplete due to its lack of clarity and depth.

Jana Nayagan fan review: Thank you, Thalapathy Some farewells end with applause. Vijay's Jana Nayagan one ends with hope, uncertainty, and millions quietly thanking a man who gave them a lifetime of memories.

What to watch on OTT this week: Exciting new releases on Netflix, Prime and more Are you up for some amazing content on OTT? This week, digital platforms are bringing a binge-worthy mix of thrill, romance, comedy and drama, ensuring your watchlist stays packed and your popcorn bowl stays busy.

The India Story review: A relevant issue lost in an underwhelming courtroom dramaRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 The India Story movie review: The India Story follows a grieving father and a novice lawyer as they challenge toxic food practices in court. The film keeps the food safety debate in focus, but its drama rarely matches the issue's urgency.

Horse riding, knives, guns: Inside Vijay Deverakonda's brutal training for Ranabaali Vijay Deverakonda underwent months of horse-riding, weapons and combat training for his next period drama, Ranabaali. The preparation was designed to make his action sequences look authentic on screen. India Today Digital has exclusive details.

Only 6 Sunny Deol films got an A rating in last 26 years. Batwara 1947 is the 7th Sunny Deol's Batwara 1947 has received an A certificate from the censor board. The rating underlines the film's Partition setting and its un-softened portrayal of violence and loss. It is also a rare film in Deol's career spanning 43 years.

Spine over career: Actor Ayesha Khan to troll warning her against protest support Dhurandhar actor Ayesha Khan was recently detained in Mumbai while trying to join students protesting over NEET irregularities. Her reply to a warning about losing Bollywood work has drawn further attention to her public support for the movement.

Musafir Cafe review: Vikrant Massey leads soulful tale of love and longingRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 Director Ruchir Arun's Musafir Cafe follows Chander, Sudha and Preeti through a shifting, unresolved love story. Its old-school emotional pull and lived-in performances give familiar tropes fresh weight. Does the show do justice to Divya Prakash Dubey's novel? Here is our review.

Jana Nayagan actor Anandhi in tears after all her scenes are cut from Vijay's film Actor Anandhi Ajay, who reportedly had a role in Jana Nayagan, said her scenes were removed from Vijay's film after nearly a year-long association. Her emotional video drew support online as the Vijay-starrer opened strongly at the box office.

Did Ramayana trailer announcement carry a hidden message about students' protest? Producer Namit Malhotra postponed the global release of the Ramayana trailer minutes before the film's San Diego Comic-Con panel. The move surprised fans and drew online speculation as student protests in Delhi remained in focus.

Tears, whistles and nostalgia: How India celebrated Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan Fans walked into theatres expecting one final celebration with Thalapathy Vijay. They walked out emotional, as Jana Nayagan transformed a festival-like first-day-first-show into a moment of reflection.

Trisha can't stop smiling after watching Vijay's Jana Nayagan on opening day Actor Trisha Krishnan attended the first day-first show of Vijay's Jana Nayagan at Chennai's Rohini Theatre. Her post-screening glimpse and the crowd outside quickly drew fans' attention online.

Love in Slow Motion review: Pyaar dosti hai, with a delayed confessionRelease Date:Thu Jul 23, 2026 What happens when the lines between friendship and romance begin to blur? Netflix's latest film, Love in Slow Motion, beautifully explores a heartfelt romance that finds beauty in the things left unsaid.

Not one word of empathy for students: Dia Mirza reacts to PM's midnight message Dia Mirza has reacted to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's late-night address on the NEET paper leak. Her reaction sharpened the debate over empathy, accountability and reform in the examination system.

Don't suppress their voices: Pooja Bhatt slams DU advisory on student protests Pooja Bhatt criticised Delhi University's advisory asking students to avoid the Jantar Mantar protests. Her intervention sharpened attention on widening student anger over the alleged NEET leak and demands for accountability.

It's good to be back: Johnny Depp returns as Scrooge in Ebenezer trailer Paramount Pictures has released the first trailer for Ebenezer, with Johnny Depp playing Ebenezer Scrooge. The film marks his first major Hollywood studio release since the 2022 Amber Heard defamation trial.

Not easy to play Ram: Yash praises Ranbir, shares Ramayana challenges at Comic-Con Actors Ranbir Kapoor and Yash joined director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra at San Diego Comic-Con 2026 to present Ramayana. Yash's praise for Ranbir's portrayal of Lord Ram and his remarks on the vision behind the project highlighted the film's ambitious global rollout.

Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya review: A warm and timely look at India's education systemRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya follows underpaid teachers navigating life inside a crumbling government school. The series uses humour and small heartbreaks to examine India's fractured education system.

The Odyssey box office Day 7: Nolan epic overtakes Oppenheimer globally Filmmaker Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is on the verge of crossing the Rs 100 crore mark in India within its first week of release. The Matt Damon-led epic has not only stayed ahead of Nolan's previous blockbuster Oppenheimer at the same stage but is also making a strong run at the North American box office.

general news.headline: CJP firm on Pradhan resignation demand, says govt sought time till tomorrowThe resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has been at the core of the month-long Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protests. The CJP said the government agreed to two of their demands. Read on to find the details.

Will he ever see again? Rahul Gandhi slams Centre over pellet gun use on studentRahul Gandhi accused the Centre of using pellet guns against students during the CJP protest, highlighting the injuries of a 19-year-old protester and demanding action against those responsible, an apology from Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.

CJP protests: India tells GitHub to block Bitchat app, Jack Dorsey leaks noticeThe Indian government has ordered GitHub to remove all repositories of Jack Dorsey's Bitchat, alleging that the Bluetooth messaging app could be used to bypass internet shutdowns and hinder investigations amid the ongoing CJP protests in Delhi.

Saudi prince found dead at luxury London hotel after party drugs, alcohol overdoseInvestigators found potentially fatal levels of gamma-hydroxybutyrate (GHB), commonly known as a party drug, along with traces of cannabis, Xanax and other anti-anxiety medications in therapeutic quantities.

The meme is mightier than the sword: Inside the art of CJP protestAt the CJP protest in Delhi's Janpath, memes, placards and painted banners have become central to the demonstration. The memes can be new, but art has always been an integral part of activism.

Jana Nayagan fan review: Thank you, ThalapathySome farewells end with applause. Vijay's Jana Nayagan one ends with hope, uncertainty, and millions quietly thanking a man who gave them a lifetime of memories.

How government scrambled in 24 hours to douse student angerWith student anger refusing to die down, the Modi government opened talks with CJP leaders and announced fast-track courts. The outreach came as the speed and scale of the unrest caught the government off guard.

Mumbai MP claims cop manhandled woman protester, shares video, police countersReferring to the backlash over the video, the Mumbai Police rejected the allegations in a post on X, saying an 'extremely irresponsible and incorrect' interpretation was being made of their employee's behaviour.

US spy satellites made India abort nuclear test. Then, Buddha smiled againTwo decades after the Pokhran-I nuclear test in 1974, India was ready to test again in 1995. But American spy satellites spotted unusual activity at Pokhran before it could. This is why PM PV Narasimha Rao withheld the final clearance, and the move helped pave the way for the successful Pokhran-II tests three years later.

Commonwealth Games 2026, Day 2 Live: India begin medal hunt with para swimmingCommonwealth Games 2026, Day 2 Live updates: India will look to add to its Commonwealth Games 2026 medal tally on Friday as action unfolds across multiple disciplines, with para powerlifting leading the medal hopes while gymnasts, swimmers, boxers and lawn bowlers also take centre stage in Glasgow.

No Metro: A nightmare for Delhi commuters but protesters find a cheat codeSeventeen Metro stations in Central Delhi have been shut on Friday over security concerns amid the Cockroach Janta Party's protest at Jantar Mantar. While demonstrators found ways around the curbs on Thursday, Delhi's office-goers and daily commuters were left navigating a traffic nightmare.

New Delhi Metro station added to closure list as total reaches 18 amid CJP protestsDelhi Metro will shut 18 stations in central Delhi from Friday morning because of security advisories linked to the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar. The move extends a second day of disruption and is expected to hit commuters as demonstrations continue in the capital.

Can't let it go on like this, Supreme Court tells Centre on NEET paper leak rowThe Supreme Court has sought an affidavit from the Centre detailing measures to prevent NEET paper leaks, while stating that it will closely monitor the investigation. The move shifts attention to permanent exam safeguards and restoring students' trust.

Live: Heard protesters' demands, we'll meet again tomorrow, say govt after meeting CJPCockroach Janta Party Nationwide Protest Live Updates: The meeting between the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) delegation and government representatives is currently underway at the Constitution Club of India in New Delhi. This came after the CJP's continued insistence that any dialogue with the government should only happen at a "neutral venue." Moreover, CJP called for a nationwide protest today (July 24), urging people to hold demonstrations in every district. The Abhijeet Dipke-led movement also said PM Modi's announcement on action against paper leaks did not address the issue of accountability or its main demand of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-related irregularities. The CJP appeared firm in its stance to continue its agitation even as Sonam Wangchuk finally broke his fast after being on a hunger strike for 26 days. The 59-year-old activist announced the end of his fast after Union Ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at the Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around Thursday midnight and conveyed the government's assurances about meeting his demands. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the CJP protest.

Musafir Cafe review: Vikrant Massey leads soulful tale of love and longingRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026Director Ruchir Arun's Musafir Cafe follows Chander, Sudha and Preeti through a shifting, unresolved love story. Its old-school emotional pull and lived-in performances give familiar tropes fresh weight. Does the show do justice to Divya Prakash Dubey's novel? Here is our review.

Bengaluru CEO rides scooter inside office to celebrate company's 'amazing quarter'A Bengaluru-based CEO rode a scooty through the NoBroker office while celebrating his Packers and Movers team's record quarter, before handing it to the winning employee in a now-viral video.

With midnight selfie video, how PM spoke to Gen Z in their own languageIn his late-night selfie video, PM Modi focused on reassuring students by promising swift legal action, fast-track courts and tougher anti-paper leak laws.

Honda unveils 10-model lineup for India: ADV160, Rebel twins, XR300 Rally, QC3 and moreSeven all-new products and three refreshed CB350 models showcased; portfolio spans ICE, EV and flex-fuel technologies as Honda doubles down on localisation

What I faced as a woman reporter covering the CJP protestPublic protests are the lifeblood of democracy, but women's safety is just as fundamental. My experience at Jantar Mantar shows that while the cause deserves attention, so do better crowd management, trained volunteers and stronger safety measures.

Insult Vande Mataram, risk jail: What is the new bill tabled in ParliamentThe new bill, tabled in Parliament on Friday by Nityanad Rai, Minister of State for Home Affairs, gives Vande Mataram the same legal protection as the national anthem, the tricolour and the Constitution.

Psychology says checking your phone first thing is more than addictionBefore your feet touch the floor, your hand reaches for your phone. For millions of people, this has become the first ritual of the day. Psychology suggests this habit is not always about phone addiction. It may also be driven by habit loops, curiosity, uncertainty and the brain's desire to reconnect with the world.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's mystery celebration decoded after historic fiftyVaibhav Sooryavanshi has explained the reason behind his mystery celebration after his historic knock during the first T20I against Zimbabwe. Sooryavanshi went past Sachin Tendulkar with his blitz that helped India win the match.

LIVE: Monsoon mayhem in Gujarat, Western Railways ops severely hitWeather in India Live Updates: After a slow start and prolonged lull over parts of western India, the southwest monsoon has made a prominent comeback. Gujarat is battling record-breaking rainfall, flash floods and widespread disruption, while heavy showers continue to lash Maharashtra, inundating low-lying areas and swelling rivers. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that the wet spell is far from over, with more heavy to very heavy rainfall expected across both states in the coming days. Follow live updates.

Not one word of empathy for students: Dia Mirza reacts to PM's midnight messageDia Mirza has reacted to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's late-night address on the NEET paper leak. Her reaction sharpened the debate over empathy, accountability and reform in the examination system.

sports.headline: Commonwealth Games 2026, Day 2 Live: India begin medal hunt with para swimming Commonwealth Games 2026, Day 2 Live updates: India will look to add to its Commonwealth Games 2026 medal tally on Friday as action unfolds across multiple disciplines, with para powerlifting leading the medal hopes while gymnasts, swimmers, boxers and lawn bowlers also take centre stage in Glasgow.

Mayank Yadav in awe of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 'unbelievable' batting Mayank Yadav believes 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the talent to reach the very top after watching him hammer his maiden international fifty. The India pacer called the teenager's fearless stroke-play "unbelievable."

Viswanathan Anand becomes interim FIDE president after EU sanctions Dvorkovich Viswanathan Anand has taken over as interim FIDE president after EU sanctions hit Arkady Dvorkovich. Anand was working as the deputy FIDE chief after Dvorkovich came into power.

Formula 1 return to India nears as FMSCI gets key government nod India's Formula 1 ambitions received a major boost after the Sports Ministry granted provisional NSF recognition to FMSCI. The move strengthens the governing body's role as the government targets an F1 return by 2028.

Italy tried to appoint Ancelotti for manager before approaching Guardiola Italy's coaching search began with an ambitious move for Carlo Ancelotti before the FIGC turned to Pep Guardiola after Brazil ruled out the veteran's departure. Paolo Maldini says the federation wanted to start with "the best in the world."

No all-out push at CWG 2026: Mirabai Chanu prioritises Asian Games Mirabai Chanu said she will not chase a 90kg snatch at the Commonwealth Games, keeping her build-up intact for the Asian Games. Chanu is aiming to secure her third consecutive gold medal at the CWG.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's mystery celebration decoded after historic fifty Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has explained the reason behind his mystery celebration after his historic knock during the first T20I against Zimbabwe. Sooryavanshi went past Sachin Tendulkar with his blitz that helped India win the match.

Could the Ashes be played in India? Cricket Australia open to lucrative idea Todd Greenberg said Cricket Australia would consider staging an Australia vs England Test in India. The financial appeal of the Indian market has been on the rise in recent times with the Big Bash League staging a match in Chennai later this year.

Commonwealth Games Lite: Why 10 sports were axed from Glasgow 2026 As the 2026 Commonwealth Games get underway in Glasgow, a drastically reduced sports list tests a new, low-cost formula to save the event—while taking away India's biggest medal-winning events.

From World No.1 to penniless: How tennis icon Arantxa Vicario became bankrupt Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has blamed former husband Josep Santacana for the loss of her fortune and the debt case that followed. The former world No. 1 says she is still repaying the bank, a fall that turned a glittering career into a fight for survival.

India at Commonwealth Games: July 24 schedule, medal events, fixtures in Glasgow India's campaign at the Commonwealth Games 2026 gathers momentum on Friday, July 24, with medal opportunities in para powerlifting and a packed schedule across gymnastics, swimming, lawn bowls and boxing in Glasgow.

Commonwealth Games declared open as Mirabai, Lovlina lead India at Parade of NationsOlympic medallists Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain led India into the Commonwealth Games 2026 opening ceremony to a thunderous reception as King Charles III officially declared the Games open at the OVO Hydro in Glasgow on Wednesday.

Childhood dream kept me motivated: Mayank Yadav 2.0 impresses on injury comebackIndia pacer Mayank Yadav has opened up on the mental toll of his two-year injury layoff, describing the prolonged absence from international cricket as the toughest phase of his young career after making an impressive return against Zimbabwe in the first T20I on Thursday.

Highlights: Lovlina, Mirabai lead India's contingent in styleCommonwealth Games 2026 Highlights: The Commonwealth Games 2026 began with a star-studded opening ceremony at Glasgow's OVO Hydro Arena. Around 3,000 athletes from 74 nations, including a strong Indian contingent led by Mirabai Chanu, will mark the start of the 11-day spectacle.

Our generation deserves every opportunity to learn: Shubman Gill on NEET protestsShubman Gill shared a message calling for compassion, mutual respect and greater opportunities for young people as student protests continue, while stressing the importance of education and peaceful expression.

PV Sindhu calls out China Open scheduling after Chen Yufei ends her campaignPV Sindhu questioned the China Open scheduling after her second-round exit on Thursday, highlighting the disparity in recovery time between players with a tongue-in-cheek post on social media following her defeat to home favourite Chen Yufei.

Hard work must be rewarded, cheating has no place: Sachin amid NEET protestsBatting great Sachin Tendulkar on Thursday called for a transparent and merit-based system amid the ongoing student protests, saying young people should never feel that their hard work has gone unrewarded.

Could not be happier: Shreyas Iyer savours his first win as India's T20I captainIND vs ZIM: Shreyas Iyer celebrated his maiden win as India's T20I captain after a dominant victory over Zimbabwe, heaping praise on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, whose record-breaking 18-ball fifty powered India to a comfortable chase.

India's Putul Sonowal stuns 2023 world champion Ryan Bester on CWG debutIndia's lawn bowls campaign at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games got off to a flying start on Thursday as Putul Sonowal produced one of the biggest upsets of the opening day, defeating 2023 world champion Ryan Bester of Canada in a tie-break in the Men's Singles.

Sooryavanshi's maiden fifty helps Shreyas Iyer register first win as India captainZIM vs IND, 1st T20I: India's seven-match winless run in T20Is came to an end in Harare on Thursday as Mayank Yadav's hostile spell and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's record-breaking half-century sealed a commanding eight-wicket win over Zimbabwe in the series opener.

Sooryavanshi pips Sachin to become youngest to score an international fiftyVaibhav Sooryavanshi scripted history by becoming the youngest player from a Full Member nation to score an international fifty. The 15-year-old surpassed Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record with a sparkling knock against Zimbabwe in the T20I in Harare.

India beat Malta in Bowls women's pairs event in Glasgow CWG campaign openerIndia began their Glasgow Commonwealth Games campaign with a tense tie-break win over Malta in the women's pairs lawn bowls event on Thursday. Rupa Rani Tirkey (lead) and Pinki (skip) won the opening set 7-1 before Malta took the second 4-3 to force a tie-break.

WTC Final: The Oval returns as host for 2027 title clashThe ICC has confirmed The Oval as the venue for the 2027 World Test Championship final in London. The decision comes as the qualification race remains open, with all nine Test nations still in contention.

IND vs ZIM: Ashok Sharma earns maiden India cap, Mayank Yadav returns after 21 monthsIND vs ZIM, 1st T20I Playing XIs: Ashok Sharma's impressive rise from IPL sensation to India international continued as the Rajasthan pacer made his T20I debut against Zimbabwe. India also welcomed back express quick Mayank Yadav after a 21-month injury-enforced absence.

Ashes 2027 schedule announced, Trent Bridge returns to host series openerThe ECB has scheduled the men's and women's Ashes to run in parallel again in 2027. Trent Bridge and Headingley will serve as the openers for the men's and women's Ashes respectively.

Commonwealth Games 2026 Opening Ceremony: Timing, TV Channel, Live StreamingThe Commonwealth Games 2026 open in Glasgow with a star-studded ceremony at the OVO Hydro. The event launches 11 days of competition and puts India in focus with Mirabai Chanu leading the contingent.

CJP Protest: Students use Norway's viral Viking Row with a twist in MumbaiThe students in Mumbai, who are part of the CJP protests, decided to use the Norway football team's iconic Viking Row as a medium to voice their concerns in Mumbai.

How Lovlina Borgohain got India CWG 2026 medal before opening ceremonyLovlina Borgohain moved straight into the Commonwealth Games 2026 women's 75kg semifinals after a bye. That put India on the medals table before the opening ceremony, with at least bronze already assured.

Indian judoka Arun Kumar cut from CWG 2026 after doping test failureIndia judoka Arun Kumar has been ruled out of the Commonwealth Games after an adverse analytical finding in an out-of-competition test. The late notice leaves India without a replacement in the men's 73kg event and strips a medal hope on the eve of competition.

Keep faith in government: Dhawan urges patience from students amid protestsShikhar Dhawan asked protesting students to stay patient and trust the government amid the CJP protests in Delhi. The statement comes after the demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and examination irregularities have continued to garner nationwide attention.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
